figures.caption: Fig. 4: Transition graph of the dataset (excluding insignificant transitions and self transitions) elucidating the probabilities of transition from one state to the other for the corresponding dataset. The nodes represent corresponding sleep stages

Table 1: Results comparison with different models, trained and tested on SleepEDF-20, SleepEDF-78, SHHS and iSLEEPS dataset, respectively. Performance of our model is shown in bold. The performance has been measured using Accuracy (ACC), Macro F1 Score (MF1) and Kappa Score (κ), which shows that the model predictions align substantially with respect to random chance.

Fig. 2: Medically Annotated Epochs from iSLEEPS showing wellfocussed and ill-focussed sleep-relevant artefacts based on the model trained on SHHS and tested on iSLEEPS. The title also includes the probability of the predicted class provided by the model in brackets.

Fig. 5: Distribution of the Average Run Length in the Datasets. The Violin Chart clearly highlights that the iSLEEPS dataset has a much wider range for the feature while SleepEDF-78 is more constrained within a smaller range of values.
